title: Top 5 Geopolitics Headlines

slides.headline: Election tension in Brazil
slides.explanation: President Lula and rival Flávio Bolsonaro are facing off over a new proposal for US tariffs. This clash highlights the heated political climate as the election approaches.

slides.headline: NASA's space rescue mission
slides.explanation: A NASA-funded robot has launched to save a falling space telescope. The mission aims to catch the telescope in orbit before it burns up in the atmosphere.

slides.headline: Tensions rise at Strait of Hormuz
slides.explanation: Iran wants to start charging a toll for ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz. This is a major sticking point in ongoing discussions related to the US-Iran war.

slides.headline: New support for assault survivors
slides.explanation: A new international support network for survivors of drug-facilitated rape has gained hundreds of members. It was started by two women who experienced these crimes personally.

slides.headline: Right-wing alliance grows in Spain
slides.explanation: Spain's conservative People's party and the far-right Vox party have formed a new coalition in Andalucía. This move suggests a potential partnership on a national level.

caption: Stay up to date with the top stories from around the globe. From space missions to international politics, here is what you need to know today. Download the Headlinne app for more. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

title: Top 5 Things In Tech Today

slides.headline: A warning on sharing kids' photos
slides.explanation: The National Crime Agency is warning parents about sharing photos of their children online. They say AI is being used to create harmful content from these public images.

slides.headline: Amazon quietly updates Fire HD 10
slides.explanation: Amazon has given its 2023 Fire HD 10 tablet a small performance boost. The 32GB model now comes with 4GB of RAM instead of 3GB.

slides.headline: Looking beyond Chrome and Safari
slides.explanation: Chrome and Safari still dominate, but new browsers are trying to change that. We are taking a look at the best alternatives available right now.

slides.headline: Anthropic moves into drug research
slides.explanation: The AI company Anthropic just launched Claude Science. It is a new workbench designed to help scientists manage data and create visuals for their research.

slides.headline: Updates from the space industry
slides.explanation: NASA has signed a deal with Rocket Lab for three new launches. Meanwhile, other companies are hitting major milestones in space travel.

caption: From new AI tools for science to important online safety warnings, here is your quick tech update for the day. Tap through to see what is happening in the world of technology right now. Stay informed with Headlinne. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
